# Governance Overhead

## Explains

Governance itself is a cost that must justify its existence through the value it creates.

## The Concept

The observer changes the observed. Every governance layer, every audit, every
compliance check consumes resources that could be spent on actual work. This is
not a peripheral concern — it is a fundamental property of governance systems.
The act of governing has a cost measured in time, attention, friction, and
opportunity.

Governance that doesn't justify its cost through reduced risk, better decisions,
or faster execution is pure overhead — it's organizational cholesterol. Layers
accumulate over time as responses to past failures, but rarely get removed when
conditions change. The result is governance that exists because it once solved a
problem that no longer exists.

This isn't an argument against governance; it's an argument for SELF-AWARE
governance that measures its own ROI. The minimum viable governance is whatever
produces more value than it consumes. Every rule, every gate, every approval
chain should be able to answer: "What value do I create that exceeds the cost I
impose?" If it can't answer, it's a candidate for retirement.

The practical implication is that governance systems need the same discipline
applied to them that they apply to the things they govern. They need metrics,
reviews, and sunset criteria. They need to earn their place continuously, not
just at the moment of creation.

## Why This Matters for Governance

JOS exists to govern — but it must govern its own weight. Every object in this
registry, every standing order, every SOP imposes cognitive and operational load
on the people and systems it touches. If JOS becomes heavier than the problems
it solves, it has failed on its own terms.

This concept is the immune system against governance bloat. It demands that
parsimony isn't just a nice-to-have but a survival requirement. Governance that
forgets this becomes the very bureaucracy it was designed to prevent.

- JOS-C02 (Requisite Parsimony)
- JOS-P11 (Everything That Matters, Only What Matters)
- JOS-SO-009 (Right thing = easiest thing)
